description_long: This sculpture features two cherubic children, each with curly hair and classical features, positioned on either side of an ornate oval medallion. The medallion itself is adorned with a relief portrait of a third face, possibly a historical or mythological figure, framed by decorative elements. The child on the left stands upright, holding a floral garland above the medallion, while the child on the right kneels, supporting the lower edge of the medallion with one arm. The entire composition rests on a rectangular stone base, which is textured and weathered, suggesting age and outdoor exposure. The figures are rendered in a realistic, classical style, with detailed drapery and expressive postures. The surface of the sculpture is predominantly gray, with subtle variations in tone due to material aging and environmental effects.
description: A classical stone sculpture of two cherubic children holding an ornate medallion with a relief portrait, set on a rectangular base. The figures are highly detailed, with realistic drapery and expressive poses.
description_view_0: The front view shows both children and the medallion clearly, highlighting the relief portrait and the floral garland held by the standing child.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the depth of the sculpture, showing the profile of the standing child and the kneeling child's back.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the textured back of the medallion and the base, with both children seen from behind, obscuring their faces.
description_view_3: The right side view focuses on the kneeling child and the side profile of the standing child, with the medallion's thickness visible.
attribute_subject_matter: two cherubic children holding a medallion with a relief portrait
attribute_location_context: No specific context is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background, suggesting it could be installed in a park, plaza, or similar outdoor urban setting.
